content: [unclear]
Hello, let us get straight into the lesson. Today we are going to look at dividing using short division. Have you done this method before?
A little bit.
Good. Short division is sometimes called the bus stop method. Do you know why?
Because of the shape it makes with the number?

Exactly. Let me remind you how to set it up. You write the number you are dividing by on the outside, and the number being divided on the inside. Let us start with 84 divided by 4. I will walk you through the first one. We start by looking at the first digit. How many times does 4 go into 8?
Twice.
Good. We write 2 above the 8. Now we look at the second digit. How many times does 4 go into 4?
Once.
So the answer is 21. Let us try 96 divided by 3. How many times does 3 go into 9?
3 times.
And how many times does 3 go into 6?
2 times.
So what is the answer?
Correct. Now let us try one where the first digit does not divide evenly. What is 78 divided by 6?
Um... 6 goes into 7 once, with 1 remainder.
Good. So we write 1 above the 7 and carry the remainder of 1 to the next digit. So now we have 18. How many times does 6 go into 18?
3 times.
So the answer is?
Correct. Let us check: 13 times 6 equals 78. That is right. Let us now try a three-digit number. What is 252 divided by 4?
[unclear] 4 goes into 2... it does not go. So we need to carry.
Good. When the divisor does not go into the first digit, we look at the first two digits together. So we look at 25. How many times does 4 go into 25?
6 times, with 1 remainder.
Correct. So we write 6 and carry the remainder 1. Now we have 12. How many times does 4 go into 12?
3 times exactly.
So the answer is?
Well done. Let us try 462 divided by 3.
3 goes into 4 once, remainder 1. Carry the 1 to make 16. 3 goes into 16... 5 times, remainder 1. Carry to make 12. 3 goes into 12 four times.
So the answer is?
Correct. Let me give you another one: 784 divided by 7.
7 goes into 7 once. No remainder. 7 goes into 8 once, remainder 1. Carry to make 14. 7 goes into 14 twice.
Answer?
Well done. Now let us look at divisions with a remainder at the end. What is 97 divided by 4?
4 goes into 9 twice, remainder 1. Carry to make 17. 4 goes into 17 four times, remainder 1.
So the answer is 24 remainder 1. Let us think about what that means in context. If you had 97 items and wanted to put them in groups of 4, you would have 24 complete groups and 1 left over.
Okay.
Let me give you some questions to try on your own. 456 divided by 6.
[unclear] 6 into 4, cannot do. 6 into 45 is 7, remainder 3. Carry to make 36. 6 into 36 is 6. Answer is 76.
Correct. And 528 divided by 8?
8 into 5, cannot do. 8 into 52 is 6, remainder 4. Carry to make 48. 8 into 48 is 6. Answer is 66.
Well done. Let us now try a four-digit number. 3,612 divided by 4.
4 into 3, cannot. 4 into 36 is 9. No remainder. 4 into 1 is 0, remainder 1. Carry to make 12. 4 into 12 is 3. Answer is 903.
Excellent. Very accurate. Let me give you one with a remainder: 4,157 divided by 6.
6 into 4, cannot. 6 into 41 is 6, remainder 5. Carry to make 55. 6 into 55 is 9, remainder 1. Carry to make 17. 6 into 17 is 2, remainder 5. Answer is 692 remainder 5.
That is correct. Very well done on that harder one. Let me ask you a word problem now. A box holds 6 items. How many boxes are needed for 258 items?
258 divided by 6 is 43.
Let me check your working. 6 into 25 is 4, remainder 1. Carry to make 18. 6 into 18 is 3. So 43 boxes. Correct.
Good.
Here is another one. Some chairs are arranged in rows of 9. There are 315 chairs in total. How many rows are there?
315 divided by 9. 9 into 31 is 3, remainder 4. Carry to make 45. 9 into 45 is 5. Answer is 35.
Correct, 35 rows. Excellent. Can you tell me the key steps of short division as a summary?
You divide the first digit or first two digits by the divisor. Write the result on top. If there is a remainder, carry it to the next digit and combine. Then repeat for each digit.
That is a great summary. Well done for today. See you next time.
Thank you. Bye.
